cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out this fruit problem. So, there are five fruits: kiwis, pears, peaches, loquats, and apples. Each has a different price, and we need to determine the order from cheapest to most expensive based on the given statements. Then, determine which option correctly identifies the second-cheapest fruit. The problem gives us these four statements: 1. The loquats are more expensive than the kiwis. So, loquats > kiwis in price. 2. The pears are more expensive than the peaches. So, pears > peaches. 3. The apples are more expensive than the loquats. Apples > loquats. 4.
